home.title: Make custom skins for League of Legends
home.tagline: Guides, tools, and workflows from the Divine Skins creator community.
home.browseSubheading: Every topic we cover, sorted into portals. Pick a lane and dive in.
home.communitySubheading: Every page has an Edit on GitHub button. Something wrong? Fix it. Know a thing we don't? Write it down.
home.tracksSubheading: Three of the most-trafficked tracks in the wiki.
home.trackWalkthroughDesc: New here? Start with the end-to-end path from blank Maya scene to a ready-to-install fantome file.
home.trackMayaDesc: Mesh swaps, rigging, weight painting, and exporting the way the Divine creators actually do it.
home.trackVfxDesc: Bin editing, particle work, and getting effects to land without crashing the game client.

draft.fieldDescriptionPlaceholder: One sentence, about 160 characters. Shown in search and previews.
draft.bodyPlaceholder: Start writing. Use the toolbar above to drop in components. Type @ to link to another guide.
draft.previewUnavailable: Preview unavailable right now. Your draft is safe and you can still publish.
draft.handoffIntro: Your guide goes to the wiki as a pull request on GitHub. Follow the three steps. Keep this tab open until you finish.
draft.stepForkBody: A fork is your own copy of the wiki on GitHub. You only do this once. Your guide is not in it yet, step 2 adds it. When the fork is ready, come back to this tab. Already have a fork? Skip to step 2.
draft.stepCreateBody: This opens a new file on your fork with your guide filled in.
draft.stepCreateBodyLong: Your guide is long, so GitHub can't pre-fill it. Copy your guide first. Then paste it into the empty file on GitHub.
draft.usernameNeeded: Type your GitHub username above to unlock this link.
draft.stepPrBody: Commit the file on GitHub. Keep the first option, commit to main. Then come back and click the button below. GitHub shows your changes, click "Create pull request". We review it and publish it.
draft.openPrOnGithub: Open the pull request on GitHub
draft.maintainerPath: Have write access to the wiki repo?
draft.handoffEditBody: GitHub can't pre-fill edits. Copy this, then paste it over the file on GitHub.
draft.imagesHeading: One more step: upload your images
draft.imagesBody: GitHub can't upload image files for you. Commit your guide first. Then upload these files to public/wiki-images/ in the same pull request:
draft.imagesOutcome: Your images show on the wiki once the pull request is merged.
draft.uploadImageBlurb: Pick a file. It shows in the preview and is referenced in your guide. Upload it to GitHub after Contribute.
draft.linkCheckBody: These names match other guides on the wiki. Click one to turn it into a link.
draft.editLoadError: Couldn't load this guide's source. Open it directly on GitHub instead.
draft.slugCollision: A guide already exists at this file name. Pick a different one or you'll overwrite it.
draft.previewEmptyBody: Start writing on the left. The preview updates as you type, exactly like the published page.

picker.pickerSubheading: Pick your path. Both end with a pull request a maintainer reviews and merges.
picker.visualEditorBody: Write in the browser with a live preview. No setup. You need a GitHub account to submit.
picker.manualGithubBody: Edit Markdown files directly on GitHub. Best for big changes and image-heavy guides.
